intent: Exclude-overlap suppression (f3 section 3.5, catalog #32). An exclude-list rule (msgtype=CWD) overlapping a recursive /etc watch's event scope. The exclude REDUCES net bytes (removes CWD sub-records); it never adds. Tests suppression-interaction modeling.

rules.note: exclude-list rule; drops CWD records from event groups. SUPPRESSIVE (reduces per-event bytes ~60 B). Contributes 0 additive volume; a naive impl counting it as additive is the trap.
RECURSIVE /etc dir watch (f3 section 2.2), write+attr. HIGH (low1k/typ20k/high200k, f3 section 6). Net per-event bytes REDUCED by the CWD exclude above (900 -> ~840 B).

suspected_trap: The exclude-list rule (-a always,exclude -F msgtype=CWD) is SUPPRESSIVE: it removes the CWD sub-record (~60 B, f3 section 3.2) from every event group, reducing net bytes where it overlaps the /etc watch. A naive impl that counts the exclude rule as ADDITIVE (as if it audits CWD) OVER-states cost; the correct net is the additive watch alone at a REDUCED per-event byte size (~840 B vs 900 B), i.e. ~$2.55/mo, <= the watch-alone $2.74/mo. Direction (suppressive, not additive) is the load-bearing assertion, paired with the recursive-watch HIGH tier.
